claimSummary: DC Water's inventory map uses verified lead suspected lead no information suspected non-lead and verified non-lead categories for parcel lookup.
reviewerNote: Official utility inventory and address lookup page reviewed manually for current parcel-map categories.

claimSummary: DC Water publishes lead unknown and galvanized-iron notification letters in multiple languages and directs customers to Lead Free DC at lead@dcwater.com or 202-787-4044 for help.
reviewerNote: Official notification page reviewed manually for multilingual letter availability and contact guidance.

claimSummary: DC Water says homeowners enrolled in the Lead Pipe Replacement Assistance Program receive a 100 percent discount on eligible private-side replacement after DOEE approval and use of an approved contractor.
reviewerNote: Official program page reviewed manually for DOEE approval and approved-contractor requirements.

claimSummary: DC Water's voluntary full replacement program pays for work in public space while the property owner pays for work on private property when both sides are lead and no capital project is scheduled.
reviewerNote: Official program page reviewed manually for public-space versus private-property cost split.

claimSummary: DC Water's FY 2024 cost page lists an estimated $21000 full replacement cost and $7200 private-side-only replacement cost and says the full estimate includes permitting traffic control paving and private-property restoration.
reviewerNote: Official cost methodology page reviewed manually.

claimSummary: Denver Water says its address map can show whether a home is one of the estimated 60000 to 64000 homes with a possible lead service line and publishes a longer replacement outlook from 2026 to 2031.
reviewerNote: Official utility inventory page reviewed manually for address-map and longer construction outlook language.

claimSummary: Denver Water says customers with identified possible lead service lines will be notified before neighborhood replacement work and that the inventory is built from records tests and inspections.
reviewerNote: Official notification and service-line explanation page reviewed manually for neighborhood replacement notice workflow.

claimSummary: Denver Water says the Lead Reduction Program replaces customer-owned lead service lines with copper at no direct charge on the utility schedule and requires owner consent before work proceeds.
reviewerNote: Official replacement program page reviewed manually for no-direct-charge replacement and owner-consent rules.

claimSummary: Denver Water says approved off-schedule replacements can receive a one-time $3800 reimbursement if the property is not on the current or next-year schedule and the owner uses the separate reimbursement workflow.
reviewerNote: Official reimbursement page reviewed manually for off-schedule eligibility and reimbursement cap.

claimSummary: Milwaukee Water Works says it is committed to replacing all 65000 remaining lead service lines by 2037 and explains that replacement work is prioritized by need and neighborhood conditions.
reviewerNote: Official Milwaukee overview page reviewed manually.

claimSummary: Milwaukee's lead pipes guidance says property owners receive notice before planned replacement work and explains when private-side costs are city-funded versus owner cost-sharing.
reviewerNote: Official city lead pipes FAQ page reviewed manually.

claimSummary: Milwaukee's prioritization page says the utility aims to replace 65000 lead service lines by 2037 and uses line density child blood lead data and socioeconomic need to prioritize neighborhoods.
reviewerNote: Official prioritization program page reviewed manually.

claimSummary: Milwaukee's Owner Request Program says eligible 1-4 unit residential owners can request replacement, the city pays the public side, and the 2025 average private-side cost is $3999 with the owner paying the lesser of actual cost or the average cost.
reviewerNote: Official owner request program page reviewed manually.

claimSummary: Lead Free SPRWS says SPRWS serves about 96000 properties, about 17000 (18 percent) have a lead service line, and about 4500 (5 percent) have a service line of unknown material.
reviewerNote: Official Lead Free SPRWS page reviewed manually.

claimSummary: SPRWS says customers should search the service line material map, and that eligible property owners can submit an intent form and will be contacted directly when no-cost replacement is scheduled in their area.
reviewerNote: Official Lead Free SPRWS page reviewed manually.

claimSummary: Lead Free SPRWS is described as a 10-year plan to replace all lead water service lines in the SPRWS service area at no cost to property owners.
reviewerNote: Official Lead Free SPRWS page reviewed manually.
